name: maritime_transportation_workplace_injury_oakline_st_lt
premise: A model-driven workflow escalated automatically; humans disagree on whether the escalation itself caused avoidable harm. In Maritime Transportation, the situation tagged internally around maritime transportation workplace injury oakline st lt has converged with local pressure in the Transportation corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Two executives have pre-committed to opposite remedies in side channels; whichever memo lands first will box everyone in. Middle management filtered an escalation; the executive on point is hearing some details for the first time on this bridge. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. The trade is not abstract: it is short term versus long term under incomplete information, and whichever value you deprioritize will have defenders who are technically correct about the risk they fear.

agents.name: Mei Park
agents.identity: I am Mei Park, the duty officer with sign-off authority for the next window in Maritime Transportation. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat long term as disposable collateral every time short term spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Mei Kowalski
agents.identity: I am Mei Kowalski, the advocate representing the population most likely to lose if shortcuts normalize in Maritime Transportation. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so short term cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes long term in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will redirect resources to the smallest cohort first where harm is most concentrated, documenting tradeoffs openly—privileging fairness dimensions of long term.
I will optimize for aggregate harm reduction across the whole population immediately—privileging the utilitarian reading of short term even where distribution gets rough.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Two incompatible drafts are circulating; only one can become official. Mei Park must choose under short term versus long term. What does Mei Park do?
